title: spawn_detached name misleads: task is still scope-bounded
description: ferrum-lang-concurrency.md §1.2 shows 's.spawn_detached(expr)' described as 'no handle returned, task still bounded by scope'. The word 'detached' universally means 'not bounded by a parent scope' (POSIX detached threads, Java daemon threads, Go goroutines). A caller reading spawn_detached will expect the task to outlive the scope. The correct name is spawn_untracked or spawn_no_handle or spawn_void. The current name will cause experienced concurrent programmers to write scope-escaping code patterns that happen not to escape because of the scope guarantee — until they reach for or_abandon and get surprised again.
